title: spec-quality
description: The verifier for spec cells in the corpus family. A spec advances to validated only by clearing this rubric. A spec asset is a SKILL-format artifact (front-matter intent surface + brief + an embedded ```json contract block + optional references/), or a legacy json-only contract — the gate reads the embedded contract either way. This rubric must itself be `validated` (demonstrated determinism + calibration) before any spec binds to it — the rubric-maturity precondition. Gate dimensions are mechanical and run by bin/spec-quality-check.py; review dimensions need a calibrated critic that is not the authoring agent.
maturity_precondition: This rubric cell must be validated (deterministic across repeated runs on fixed input; calibrated against the few-shot exemplar set) before it gates any spec.

calibration.critic_model: an independent critic tier, NOT the spec-authoring agent
recalibration_triggers: a false-pass observed in the ledger
a new spec layer/scope enters the corpus family
calibration.note: Few-shot scored specs spanning the scale (a strong spec, a prose-only-criteria spec, an unbounded-scope spec, an unsound-decomposition near-miss); periodic agreement checks against reference scores. An uncalibrated judge drifts generous toward model-authored specs.

pristine_reference.note: For decomposition scoring, the parent spec's acceptance-criteria list is the reference the decomposition is scored against. It is supplied read-only and the authoring agent cannot edit it — a pristine reference the worker cannot reach.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: Conforms to the spec/intent schema; cell id excludes maturity; all cross-references resolve.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Not instantiable as a typed cell.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: Every acceptance criterion is either executable or bound to a calibrated review rubric. Zero prose-only criteria.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Uncheckable criteria are hopes, not criteria; the loop cannot converge on them.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: A rubric cell binds to this spec WITHOUT reinterpretation, and that rubric cell is itself `validated`.
dimensions.mechanized_by: bin/spec-quality-check.py (rubric-binds: a binds_rubric pointing at a rubric.* cell)
dimensions.fail_meaning: A spec a rubric cannot bind to scores against vibes downstream.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.fail_meaning: Unbounded scope; the spec admits work it was never meant to authorize.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: When the spec is authored in SKILL form (front-matter present), the skill surface and the machine contract AGREE: `name` present, `description` (the intent surface) present, and the contract `cell`'s slug == `name`. A legacy json-only spec passes vacuously — valid but minimal.
dimensions.fail_meaning: A spec whose human-facing intent surface and machine contract disagree is two specs wearing one name — the reader and the loop see different things.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: _entailment_check.py passes: every parent acceptance criterion is covered by >=1 child cell; every child acceptance binds to a rubric cell; no orphan criteria; dependency edges respect the layer partial order. This is a PROPERTY check (entailment under the partial order), not a surface extensional check — harder to fake than to satisfy.
dimensions.mechanized_by: bin/spec-quality-check.py → bin/_entailment_check.py (when the spec declares a decomposition)
dimensions.fail_meaning: Satisfying the children would not entail satisfying the parent — an unsound decomposition that ships gaps.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Does the spec capture the principal's actual want? Is every claim grounded in the intent source, not invented?
dimensions.fail_meaning: Fuzzy or drifted intent at the root; multiplied downstream.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Every boundary in the spec answers: what crosses it, in what form, under whose authority, and what happens when it fails.
dimensions.fail_meaning: An underspecified boundary is a future production incident.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Terms used as defined in the ontology; new terms flagged; no undefined vocabulary.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Imprecise vocabulary lets the how dictate the what and corrupts decomposition.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Target state, invariants, preconditions, and postconditions are unambiguous (Design-by-Contract complete).
dimensions.fail_meaning: Ambiguous commitment yields thrashing or unverifiable doneness.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Beyond mere coverage: is this the RIGHT carving of the problem across scope and layer, or a covering that will mislead?
dimensions.fail_meaning: A technically-covering but wrong decomposition tunnels the build toward a bad structure.

scoring.gates: ALL gate dimensions must pass; any gate failure returns the spec to regenerating regardless of review score. bin/spec-quality-check.py runs the gate dimensions mechanically and exits 0 iff all pass.
scoring.review: Weighted review score must clear each dimension threshold AND an aggregate of 80/100.
scoring.signal: On pass, the CRITIC (not the authoring agent) writes signals/{spec-cell}/{ts}--spec-quality.json via the validation path. signals/ is deny-on-write to the worker (gate-signal); the authoring agent cannot write the signal that validates its own spec.
